title: Jury Duty

jokes.text: Stress is like the uptight mayor of a town who's saying 'Hey, we're uptight You can't dance.' And then, you have to be like 'Oh, yeah, Mayor Stress? Well, watch this.' And then, we dance.
jokes.setup: Andy's overly elaborate metaphor for dealing with stress
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of arguing with personified stress and responding with dancing
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy - overly complicated metaphor delivered with complete sincerity. The personification of stress as a mayor is genuinely creative and character-specific.

jokes.text: Sex also works
jokes.setup: Following the elaborate stress-dancing metaphor
jokes.punchline: Casual, matter-of-fact addition that completely undercuts the previous statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and economy. Two words that completely deflate Andy's philosophical moment with blunt honesty.

jokes.text: Hey! Tuna He's back
jokes.setup: Jim returning from supposed jury duty
jokes.punchline: Andy's enthusiasm and fish nickname for Jim
jokes.explanation: Standard Andy enthusiasm with his established nickname pattern. Reliable character comedy.

jokes.text: Tuna wrap Okay... Hand roll
jokes.setup: Continuing the tuna nickname theme
jokes.punchline: Andy extending the fish metaphor to different preparations
jokes.explanation: Andy being Andy - taking a joke too far with fish preparations. Mildly clever wordplay.

jokes.text: Makes me question the whole judicial system
jokes.setup: Learning Jim served on a jury
jokes.punchline: Andy's dramatic overreaction to Jim's civic participation
jokes.explanation: Andy's tendency to make everything about himself and overdramatize situations.

jokes.text: (MOCKING) He apologized And I just really want him to like me. Mmm!
jokes.setup: Jim saying the defendant was found not guilty
jokes.punchline: Andy's mocking impression of Jim as overly sympathetic
jokes.explanation: Standard office mockery. Andy's jealousy showing through his teasing.

jokes.text: We're best friends actually. We're going wine tasting next weekend if you want to come
jokes.setup: Andy mocking Jim for being too sympathetic to defendant
jokes.punchline: Jim escalating the fictional relationship with deadpan delivery
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim - using deadpan escalation to make Andy's mockery backfire. Well-structured setup/payoff.

jokes.text: And then three other days happened
jokes.setup: Jim explaining taking extra days off to help Pam
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that he kept taking more days
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim delivery - matter-of-fact admission of guilt with minimal words. The phrase 'days happened' is beautifully passive.

jokes.text: Is it black? Because that would be hilarious
jokes.setup: Learning Angela had her baby
jokes.punchline: Kevin's inappropriate and tactless question
jokes.explanation: Pure Kevin cringe - inappropriate comment that's funny because it's so wrong and so Kevin.

jokes.text: Would it be more hilarious than Angela having a Chinese baby?
jokes.setup: Kevin's inappropriate racial comment
jokes.punchline: Oscar making it worse by engaging with the premise
jokes.explanation: Oscar surprisingly engaging with Kevin's inappropriate logic, making the cringe worse.

jokes.text: A little bit. Yeah. Right, guys? Back me up
jokes.setup: Oscar engaging with racial humor premise
jokes.punchline: Kevin seeking validation for his inappropriate observation
jokes.explanation: Kevin doubling down and seeking group approval for inappropriate humor - classic Kevin move.

jokes.text: Right month wrong year
jokes.setup: Kevin asking if he won the baby pool
jokes.punchline: Oscar's dry correction about the timing being way off
jokes.explanation: Efficient joke construction - implies Kevin's guess was hilariously wrong by a whole year.

jokes.text: Yeah, Oscar's the dad I'm Oscar's dad and Angela's my mom
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining accounting as a family
jokes.punchline: The confused family structure Kevin describes
jokes.explanation: Kevin's childlike logic creating an impossible family tree. Pure Kevin confusion.

jokes.text: It's the perfect blend of love and horror
jokes.setup: Discussion about visiting Angela and the new baby
jokes.punchline: Creed's disturbing perspective on newborns
jokes.explanation: Perfectly creepy Creed line - unexpectedly poetic while being unsettling. Great character integration.

jokes.text: My wife got to the TV first. I had to sit through damn Rizzoli & damn Isles
jokes.setup: Stanley complaining about Jim's absence affecting his work
jokes.punchline: Stanley's specific grievance about having to watch a procedural show
jokes.explanation: Stanley's domestic frustration bleeding into work complaints. The specific show reference adds authenticity.

jokes.text: we used to recreate the various stranglings with empanadas from Ernesto's
jokes.setup: Toby sharing his jury duty experience
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning recreating murders with food
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and unexpected. The casual way Toby mentions using food to reenact murders is perfectly disturbing.

jokes.text: You mean food truck Ernesto's was a food truck
jokes.setup: Others talking fondly about Ernesto's restaurant
jokes.punchline: Toby's correction that deflates their nostalgia
jokes.explanation: Classic Toby - being technically correct in a way that kills the mood. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Toby, shut your hole about the empanadas Nobody cares about the empanadas
jokes.setup: Toby interrupting with food truck correction
jokes.punchline: Michael's aggressive dismissal of Toby's contribution
jokes.explanation: Pure Michael hatred for Toby. The phrase 'shut your hole' is aggressively dismissive in classic Michael fashion.

jokes.text: Got that, bimbo? Got it, bimbo
jokes.setup: Gabe calling Oscar 'bimbo' about baby protocol
jokes.punchline: Oscar playing along with the insult
jokes.explanation: Gabe's awkward attempt at authority and Oscar's resigned compliance. Mild workplace dynamic humor.

jokes.text: Philip is so fat
jokes.setup: Meeting Angela's 'premature' baby
jokes.punchline: Kevin's inappropriate but accurate observation
jokes.explanation: Kevin stating the obvious inappropriately. Simple but effective character comedy.

jokes.text: You warned me not to say anything if it was tiny, Oscar You didn't prepare me for a big, giant, fat baby
jokes.setup: Oscar's earlier warning about premature baby sensitivity
jokes.punchline: Kevin's logic that he wasn't prepared for the opposite scenario
jokes.explanation: Kevin's twisted logic is actually sound - he was warned about one scenario but faced the opposite. Good callback structure.

jokes.text: He's more than substantial He's a monster
jokes.setup: Previous comments about the baby's size
jokes.punchline: Kevin escalating from 'substantial' to 'monster'
jokes.explanation: Kevin's continued inappropriate escalation about baby's appearance. Predictable but in-character.

jokes.text: you never said he was on a bicycle
jokes.setup: Dwight questioning Jim's hit-and-run story details
jokes.punchline: Catching Jim in an inconsistency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight detective work - methodical questioning that reveals Jim's lie. Well-structured gotcha moment.

jokes.text: You have the right to request judgment by combat Dwight's rights
jokes.setup: Dwight making a citizen's arrest on Jim
jokes.punchline: Adding medieval trial by combat to Miranda rights
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Dwight - mixing modern law enforcement with medieval combat. Creative and character-specific absurdity.

jokes.text: I don't need to take a chill pill... Down the hatch
jokes.setup: Andy telling Dwight to take a chill pill
jokes.punchline: Dwight literally taking an actual pill
jokes.explanation: Dwight taking idioms literally for physical comedy. Classic character-based visual gag.

jokes.text: (SPEAKING CALMLY) But let's just say that Jim was lying about jury duty
jokes.setup: Dwight taking the 'chill pill' and calming down
jokes.punchline: Dwight continuing his investigation but now in a calm voice
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the chill pill gag - Dwight's obsession continues but now in zen mode.

jokes.text: No one's ever lied to me before
jokes.setup: Jim confessing to lying about jury duty
jokes.punchline: Andy's naive claim about never being lied to
jokes.explanation: Andy's innocence/naivety on display. Obviously untrue but delivered with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: They used to call me King Tut because I'm so good at keeping things under wraps
jokes.setup: Andy promising to keep Jim's secret
jokes.punchline: Elaborate pun connecting Egyptian pharaoh to secrecy
jokes.explanation: Decent pun from Andy, though the setup feels slightly forced. Fits his character's wordplay tendencies.

jokes.text: My nickname was actually King Butt because I had a king-sized butt
jokes.setup: Andy's impressive King Tut nickname story
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation revealing the real, embarrassing reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy self-own. Sets up impressive backstory then immediately undercuts it with embarrassing truth.

jokes.text: It's the empanada guy
jokes.setup: Ernesto arriving and Toby recognizing him
jokes.punchline: Jim's casual identification confirming the earlier story
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that confirms Toby's earlier bizarre story was true.

jokes.text: He remembers Toby the most forgettable man in the known world
jokes.setup: Ernesto remembering Toby but not Jim
jokes.punchline: Dwight's observation about the irony of Toby being memorable
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation that perfectly captures Toby's paradoxical nature - forgettable yet somehow memorable.

jokes.text: Usually I'm a burrito guy but if you won't tell, I won't. Wink, wink
jokes.setup: Andy trying empanadas for the first time
jokes.punchline: Andy treating food preference like a secret affair
jokes.explanation: Andy's tendency to make mundane things seem secretive or conspiratorial.

jokes.text: Hey, Murder, She Wrote, how about we drop the whole Murder, She Wrote thing, okay?
jokes.setup: Dwight continuing to investigate Jim's story
jokes.punchline: Jim comparing Dwight to the detective show character
jokes.explanation: Apt comparison of Dwight's detective behavior to classic TV sleuth. Self-aware humor about the situation.

jokes.text: Wait, wait, you live by the courthouse You drove from near the courthouse out to Jim's house and then back to the courthouse? Thirty minutes out 30 minutes back easy hour
jokes.setup: Andy's cover story about driving Jim to courthouse
jokes.punchline: Dwight exposing the logical flaw in Andy's story
jokes.explanation: Dwight's analytical mind catching Andy in a poorly thought out lie. Good detective work meets bad covering up.

jokes.text: And I would watch that cute, little tooshy scurry up those courthouse stairs every morning and that was that
jokes.setup: Andy trying to convince Dwight he drove Jim to court
jokes.punchline: Andy's inappropriate sexualized comment about watching Jim
jokes.explanation: Andy making the lie worse by adding inappropriate details. Cringe humor from desperation.

jokes.text: I'm not Rumpelstiltskin, Jim, I can't keep spinning gold out of your (BLEEP)
jokes.setup: Andy struggling to maintain cover story for Jim
jokes.punchline: Creative fairytale metaphor for Andy's frustration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor from Andy - comparing covering for Jim to spinning straw into gold. Creative and perfectly frustrated.

jokes.text: I'm going to call him Little Kevin
jokes.setup: Kevin holding Angela's large baby
jokes.punchline: Kevin wanting to name the baby after himself
jokes.explanation: Kevin's self-centered impulse to name other people's babies after himself. Pure Kevin logic.

jokes.text: Is he really five pounds? Because I squat with five pounds... Yeah, this feels like more than that
jokes.setup: Kevin testing the baby's supposed weight
jokes.punchline: Kevin using his workout routine as a reference point
jokes.explanation: Kevin's practical approach to fact-checking - using his gym experience to debunk the baby's claimed weight.

jokes.text: I thought you said five You know what? I was under so many drugs a James Taylor concert or something
jokes.setup: Confusion about the baby's weight
jokes.punchline: Angela's random comparison to a James Taylor concert
jokes.explanation: Angela's confused post-birth state leading to random pop culture reference. Mildly absurd.

jokes.text: Preemie pajamas... No, I think he came early just so he could wear these
jokes.setup: Pam bringing inappropriately small pajamas for the large baby
jokes.punchline: Angela's gracious response suggesting the baby timed his birth for the outfit
jokes.explanation: Angela being gracious about an awkward gift by creating a sweet fiction. Nice character moment.

jokes.text: This baby is clearly not premature They're lying about the date it was conceived And her husband's gay I don't even know which thread to follow
jokes.setup: Oscar analyzing all the evidence about Angela's situation
jokes.punchline: Oscar overwhelmed by multiple conspiracy theories
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar - analytical mind short-circuiting from too much drama to process. Great character work.

jokes.text: What? Funny? No? Nobody laughed
jokes.setup: Jim expecting laughter after confessing to lying about jury duty
jokes.punchline: Jim's awkward realization that nobody finds it funny
jokes.explanation: Jim misjudging the room's reaction to his confession. Good cringe moment.

jokes.text: So long, clump mate I'm going to miss you Truth be told, I never thought that this was how it was going to end
jokes.setup: Dwight believing Jim is about to be fired
jokes.punchline: Dwight's overly dramatic farewell to his office nemesis
jokes.explanation: Dwight showing unexpected sentiment and respect for Jim as his worthy adversary. Great character depth.

jokes.text: That's it? This is crap
jokes.setup: Andy giving Jim a mild reprimand instead of firing him
jokes.punchline: Dwight's anticlimactic disappointment
jokes.explanation: Dwight's deflation after building up to Jim's 'firing' only to get a finger wag. Good payoff.

jokes.text: The only premature baby in this room is the baby this baby ate
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether the baby is actually premature
jokes.punchline: Kevin's disturbing cannibal baby theory
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Kevin line - takes baby size joke to disturbing cannibalistic conclusion. Perfectly unexpected and quotable.

jokes.text: Philip was conceived nine months ago... we had just seen Thor, and there was way too much wine in my chicken piccata
jokes.setup: Angela finally admitting the timeline truth
jokes.punchline: Angela blaming Thor movie and wine-soaked Italian food for her pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Angela's prim way of explaining her moment of passion - blaming external factors rather than desire.

jokes.text: Why? I know what Angela and the Senator look like I can mash that up in my head right now
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting Dwight should see the baby
jokes.punchline: Dwight's practical approach to imagining what a baby would look like
jokes.explanation: Dwight's logical, efficient approach to baby-seeing. The phrase 'mash that up' is perfectly Dwight.

jokes.text: What a beautiful child Prominent forehead, short arms, tiny nose. You will lead millions willingly or as slaves
jokes.setup: Dwight finally seeing Angela's baby
jokes.punchline: Dwight's ominous prophecy about the baby's future
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - starts with normal baby talk then veers into dystopian leadership prophecy. Brilliantly character-specific.

jokes.text: That baby is a Schrute, and unless somebody taught Mose sex that baby is mine
jokes.setup: Dwight realizing the baby might be his
jokes.punchline: Dwight's crude logic about Schrute genetics and Mose's virginity
jokes.explanation: Dwight's twisted family genetics logic and casual reference to Mose's sexual inexperience. Dark but funny.

jokes.text: JIM: Yes, I am bringing my kids in to help me get out of this hole
jokes.setup: Jim bringing his family to the office
jokes.punchline: Jim openly admitting he's using his children as sympathy props
jokes.explanation: Jim's rare moment of complete honesty about his manipulation tactics. Self-aware and strategic.

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa Game face, baby. Game face... No, that's overdoing it I think. Hi, Stanley. Split the difference
jokes.setup: Jim coaching Pam on how to act for their sympathy ploy
jokes.punchline: Jim micromanaging Pam's performance level with Stanley
jokes.explanation: Jim and Pam as a manipulative team, with Jim directing the performance. Good meta-comedy about their scheme.

jokes.text: so she brought you a little treat... No. But that would have been a really good idea
jokes.setup: Cece wanting to thank everyone with a treat
jokes.punchline: Jim and Pam realizing they should have brought actual treats
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection that sets up disappointment. Jim and Pam's parenting improvisation showing.

jokes.text: She says no to everything. You know, she thinks my name is 'No.'
jokes.setup: Cece saying no when asked if she drew the pictures
jokes.punchline: Jim's parental observation about toddler behavior
jokes.explanation: Relatable parenting humor about toddler negativity phase. Genuine and well-observed.

jokes.text: Why am I shorter than the table that I'm standing next to?
jokes.setup: Andy looking at Cece's drawing of him
jokes.punchline: Andy's confusion about his proportional representation
jokes.explanation: Andy's literal interpretation of child art and concern about his depicted height.

jokes.text: Then I inserted my penis into your... No! Stop it! Dwight ...vagina
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to prove he and Angela were together
jokes.punchline: Dwight's clinical description despite Angela's protests
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight - utterly clinical and inappropriate description of intimacy. Pure cringe factor.

jokes.text: Nurse! You know that baby in there? Baby Philip? Cancel the circumcision
jokes.setup: Dwight believing the baby is his son
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately taking medical authority over the baby
jokes.explanation: Dwight immediately asserting paternal authority in the most awkward way possible. Perfect character escalation.

jokes.text: Kids drive us dads crazy. Sometimes I feel like they're raising us. Am I right?
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining why he's letting Jim off the hook
jokes.punchline: Dwight's sudden paternal wisdom and solidarity
jokes.explanation: Dwight's instant transformation into understanding father figure. Good character growth moment.

tmdb_overview: Jim returns from jury duty, only to have to deal with Dwight's pesky questions about the details of the case. Elsewhere, Angela and her senator beau welcome their baby.
